raw_bit_direct_load.method: U-Boot uEnv override + fpga loadb on FAT-root raw system.bit
raw_bit_direct_load.note: This is still the strongest proof path for arbitrary raw .bit replacement because U-Boot prints the Xilinx bitstream header fields before Linux starts. On this board, every non-stock raw candidate that actually reached PL also left AD9361 unable to complete calibration.

manual_uart_fpga_load.method: Stop autoboot on COM6, run load mmc + fpga load manually, then boot Linux with bootm
manual_uart_fpga_load.note: This is the correct validation path for .bit.bin-style payloads. Unlike the stock recovery uEnv.txt, it does not silently fall back to the BOOT.bin-resident PL image when a candidate is rejected or ignored.

validation.note: U-Boot rejects the Bootgen-converted course overlay before Linux starts, so the later healthy AD9361 state comes from the untouched stock PL shell.

rejected_external_materials.note: This external material is useful as a historical reference, but it diverges from the vendor reference XSA at axi_ad9361 feature-disable parameters and at multiple PS7 clock/reset settings, so it is not a clean course baseline candidate.

engineering_conclusion.boot_reference: There are now two distinct external PL validation paths on this board: raw .bit plus fpga loadb proves arbitrary PL replacement but every successful non-stock raw candidate still breaks AD9361, while manual UART fpga load is the correct path for .bit.bin payloads and currently accepts only two known cases tested here: the stock BOOT.bin partition payload passes cleanly, and the source-correlated vendor reference payload still fails AD9361.
engineering_conclusion.next_step: Treat stock_system_top_from_BOOT.bin as the only externally loaded boot-safe baseline for now. The next bring-up task is to explain why the extracted stock partition survives manual fpga load while the source-correlated vendor reference payload does not, then reproduce those acceptance and clean-boot properties in an editable shell before resuming gpreg/BPSK RF discovery.
